premise: I withdrew the last $85,000 from my 403(b) to fund my grandson’s fitness‑tech startup, shaking hands on a 10% equity stake; once his app hit 500,000 downloads, he issued a new share class that diluted me to zero and blocked my calls. I registered the app’s name as a competing domain, built a clone with the original source code I’d backed up, and when he sued, I countersued for fraud — the settlement awarded me full ownership of the app, I rebranded it ‘Grandma’s Gains,’ now valued at $1.2 million, and he works my customer‑service desk on minimum wage.
